analysis.consistency: High. The mechanics described (creation, movement, sensory link, interaction limitations) are internally consistent for an illusion spell focused on remote viewing and projection.
analysis.quality: High. The spell is well-written, clearly defining its strengths (long range, perfect mimicry, remote sensory access) and weaknesses (fragility, concentration requirement, sensory deprivation on the caster). The material component cost adds a nice balancing element for a high-level illusion.

mechanics.components: V, S, M (a small replica of you made from materials worth at least 5 gp)
mechanics.effect_summary: Creates a perfectly illusory, intangible duplicate of the caster at a previously visited location within 500 miles. The caster can use an action to move the illusion and can perceive the world through the illusion's senses (granting blindness/deafness to the caster's real location as a trade-off). The illusion breaks on taking any damage.
mechanics.balance_assessment: Level 7 seems appropriate for the range and the powerful reconnaissance capabilities, balanced by the required concentration and the instant destruction upon taking damage. The requirement of a 5 gp material component is notable for a consumable-like cost associated with the effect's duration.

analysis.suggested_changes: None required; the spell is functionally sound and well-scoped for a high-level slot. The mechanical consequence of switching senses (caster becomes blinded/deafened) is a strong and necessary balancing feature.
